vulnerability.title: Enable Certmap in SSSD
vulnerability.description: SSSD should be configured to verify the certificate of the user or group. To set this up ensure that section like certmap/testing.test/rule_name is setup in /etc/sssd/sssd.conf. For example [certmap/testing.test/rule_name] matchrule =<SAN>.*EDIPI@mil maprule = (userCertificate;binary={cert!bin}) domains = testing.test
vulnerability.recommendation: Review and remediate: Enable Certmap in SSSD

triage_decision.reason: Enabling certmap in SSSD is a standard authentication configuration change that follows STIG hardening patterns and is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Incorrect certmap syntax may cause authentication failures

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for sssd_enable_certmap (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sssd_enable_certmap). Could not verify.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly identifies the need to add a certmap section, which would enable certificate verification for users/groups. However, it omits several operational steps that are important for a reliable and secure configuration. A more complete plan would verify domain names, apply proper permissions, and reload the service after changes. The proposed section appears syntactically correct as long as the domain values match the system’s current configuration.
concerns: No explicit file permissions adjustment (should be 0600 for /etc/sssd/sssd.conf).
No service reload or restart is mentioned to apply changes.
Domain name in the certmap section may not match the actual configured domain, potentially rendering the rule ineffective.
Missing validation of the matchrule format (e.g., ensuring proper escaping of special characters).
suggested_improvements: Add a step to reload/ restart sssd service (e.g., systemctl reload sssd).
Verify that the domain name in the certmap section matches an existing domain in sssd.conf.
Use quotation marks around the matchrule or escape special regex characters as needed.
Include a test step (e.g., ldapsearch or sssd test) to confirm that certificate mapping is active.
